items.title: Critical Check Point VPN Zero-Day CVE-2026-50751 Actively Exploited by Qilin Ransomware
items.summary: Check Point disclosed CVE-2026-50751 on June 8, 2026 — a critical authentication bypass in Remote Access VPN, Mobile Access, and Spark Firewall products. Rapid7 confirmed active exploitation in the wild, with attackers retrieving ELF payloads from attacker-controlled servers post-exploitation. The campaign has been attributed to the Qilin ransomware operation.
items.why: A critical VPN auth-bypass actively weaponised by a ransomware group means any unpatched Check Point gateway is a direct path to full network compromise. Patch immediately and audit VPN logs from June 7 onward.

items.title: Google Chrome Zero-Day CVE-2026-5281 (Dawn WebGPU Use-After-Free) Enables RCE via Malicious Web Page
items.summary: A use-after-free flaw in Dawn, Chrome's open-source WebGPU implementation, allows remote code execution when a user visits a crafted HTML page. The bug affects all Chromium-based browsers. Google confirmed active in-the-wild exploitation and shipped an emergency patch.
items.why: Browser RCE zero-days are top-tier attack primitives. Since the entire Chromium ecosystem is affected, the exposed attack surface spans billions of endpoints — update Chrome, Edge, and Brave now.

items.title: APT28 Weaponizes Microsoft Office 1-Day CVE-2026-21509 Within 24 Hours in 9-Country European Military Campaign
items.summary: Russia's Fancy Bear (APT28) ran a concentrated 72-hour spear-phishing campaign January 28-30, 2026, sending 29 distinct emails across nine Eastern European nations targeting military and government entities. The group exploited a newly disclosed Microsoft Office vulnerability (CVE-2026-21509) within 24 hours of its public release and routed C2 traffic through cloud infrastructure to blend in.
items.why: APT28 turning around a 1-day exploit within 24 hours against NATO-adjacent militaries signals a dramatic acceleration in Russian offensive cyber tempo. Defenders must treat patch SLAs for Office vulns as hours, not weeks.

items.title: Iranian APT Screening Serpens Deploys 6 Novel RAT Variants Across 5 Countries Since February 2026
items.summary: Palo Alto Unit 42 is tracking Iran-nexus APT Screening Serpens, which has deployed six new remote access Trojan variants across entities in up to five countries since a regional conflict escalated in February 2026. The group uses job-seeker social engineering lures crafted to deceive targets in key strategic sectors.
items.why: Six distinct RAT variants in under four months points to a well-resourced tooling pipeline. The job-lure vector is extremely effective against cleared employees; HR and recruiting teams are a high-priority phishing target.

items.title: Mandiant M-Trends 2026: PROMPTFLUX and PROMPTSTEAL Malware Queries LLMs Mid-Execution to Dynamically Evade Detection
items.summary: Mandiant's M-Trends 2026 report — drawn from 500,000+ hours of frontline IR — documents two new malware families: PROMPTFLUX and PROMPTSTEAL, which actively query large language models during runtime to adapt their behaviour and bypass security controls. The report also names 'distillation attacks' — extracting proprietary logic from AI models — as an emerging intellectual-property threat.
items.why: Malware that rewrites its own behaviour in real time by calling an LLM breaks the fundamental assumption of signature and heuristic detection. This is the clearest documented case yet of AI being weaponised inside an active payload.
